description: Domestic structure adapted as a worship space, including a dedicated baptistery with early Christian wall paintings including Adam and Eve and the Good Shepherd.
significance: Oldest known purpose-built Christian worship space with iconographic program; baptistery predates all known basilica-type churches.
discovery_notes: Excavated 1920s-1930s by Yale/French teams; baptistery panels now at Yale University Art Gallery.
uncertainty: Exact date of adaptation debated within mid-3rd century horizon.

description: Synagogue with extensive figurative wall paintings providing context for late antique visual culture alongside nearby Christian sites.
significance: Key evidence for Jewish figurative art and its influence on early Christian iconographic traditions.
discovery_notes: Excavated alongside the house church; murals now at National Museum of Damascus.
uncertainty: Iconographic interpretation and liturgical use still debated.

description: Floor mosaic inscriptions within a Roman military base, including a dedicatory inscription to "God Jesus Christ" and a table described as an altar.
significance: Potentially the earliest Christian mosaic inscription yet found; uniquely attests both "altar" terminology and a female donor.
discovery_notes: Discovered 2005 during prison construction at Megiddo; mosaic panels preserved in situ.
uncertainty: Chronology and interpretation actively debated; some scholars date later.

description: Imperial Constantinian basilica complex enclosing traditional sites of crucifixion and resurrection, built by order of Constantine following Helena's pilgrimage.
significance: Primary Christian pilgrimage site since 4th century; key to development of pilgrimage theology and liturgy.
discovery_notes: Helena's pilgrimage ~326-328; Macarius of Jerusalem oversaw construction; Eusebius describes dedication.
uncertainty: Multiple rebuild phases; earliest fabric largely obscured by later construction.

description: Imperial-era basilica at Bethlehem tied to the nativity pilgrimage tradition; repeatedly rebuilt and restored.
significance: One of the oldest continuously operating churches in the world; Constantinian foundation phase documented.
discovery_notes: Built under Constantine; Justinian-era rebuild is the primary surviving structure.
uncertainty: Surviving fabric reflects multiple phases, especially post-6th-century reconstruction.

description: Multi-level Christian burial complex on the Appian Way housing crypts of early Roman bishops and martyrs including papal crypt.
significance: Key site for funerary art, martyr veneration, and episcopal commemoration in Rome; source for early Christian iconography.
discovery_notes: Rediscovered by Giovanni Battista de Rossi in 1849; ongoing excavation and documentation.
uncertainty: Dating and attribution of specific chambers varies by excavation phase.

description: Domestic-to-assembly structure in Capernaum frequently cited in discussions of early house-church development and pilgrimage memory.
significance: One of the earliest claimed Christian worship sites; demonstrates the domestic-church-to-basilica transformation.
discovery_notes: Excavated by Franciscan archaeologists; octagonal church built over domestic structure in 5th century.
uncertainty: Identification with Peter's house is interpretive and not universally accepted.

description: Armenian ecclesial center traditionally founded by Gregory the Illuminator following Armenia's adoption of Christianity; seat of the Catholicos.
significance: Symbolic center of Armenian Christianity; among the earliest state-sponsored church buildings.
discovery_notes: Archaeological investigation of earliest phases ongoing; current structure largely medieval.
uncertainty: Precise early construction phases uncertain; tradition strong but archaeology complex.

description: Anchoritic and cenobitic monastic community in the Eastern Desert of Egypt associated with Antony the Great.
significance: Foundation of Egyptian monastic tradition; influenced Benedict's Rule and Western monasticism; oldest functioning monastery.
discovery_notes: Still functioning; Athanasius' Life of Antony is the primary literary source for early occupation.
uncertainty: Current architecture spans centuries beyond initial monastic occupation.

description: Large pilgrimage complex around the pillar site of Simeon Stylites, reflecting the institutionalization of ascetic charisma.
significance: One of the largest church buildings in late antiquity; demonstrates the pilgrimage economy.
discovery_notes: UNESCO World Heritage tentative list; extensive archaeological documentation.
uncertainty: Extant ruins document mature pilgrimage phases more than initial occupation.

description: Northern Adriatic episcopal center with important mosaic floors and evidence for post-Constantinian Christian monumentalization.
significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site; earliest mosaic programs demonstrate Christian iconographic development.
discovery_notes: Excavated multiple phases; 4th-century floor mosaics remarkably well preserved.
uncertainty: Dating of some mosaic programs refined continuously through archaeology.

description: Pilgrimage church and mosaic complex in the Transjordan commemorative landscape associated with Moses.
significance: Key site for understanding late antique pilgrimage and memorial church architecture in the Holy Land.
discovery_notes: Excavated by Franciscan Biblical Institute; restored and reopened 2016.
uncertainty: Site interpretation relies on combining textual pilgrimage accounts with archaeological phases.
